known_issues: Settings page shows 'not authenticated' error when user tries to use settings features
Change password functionality is missing or not visible on the site

existing_features.title: Fix the Settings page so that authenticated users (logged in via the session auth system) are correctly recognised as authenticated and can use all settings features without seeing an 'not authenticated' or 'login required' error. The Settings page must read auth state from useSessionAuth (not Internet Identity directly) and gate UI only when the session token is genuinely absent.
Add an 'Other' option to the gender selection field on the Settings page, alongside any existing gender options (e.g. Male, Female). The value stored for this option should be 'Other'.

Add a 'Change Password' section to the Settings page. Since the app uses its own session-based username/password authentication (useSessionAuth with localStorage), expose a form that lets the authenticated user enter their current password, a new password, and a confirmation, then updates the stored password in the session auth system. Show a success or error toast on completion.

projectName: Fix Settings authentication, add 'Other' gender option, and surface change password UI
